# Phase 10.0.7 Legitimacy Trust Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated legitimacy scoring systems, trust-manipulation systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define how civilization preserves trust, legitimacy, credibility, transparency, and institutional confidence across extreme long horizons.

## Scope

- Constitutional legitimacy preservation doctrine.
- Institutional trust continuity philosophy.
- Governance transparency survivability standards.
- Civilization credibility preservation architecture.
- Public trust continuity doctrine.
- Constitutional legitimacy verification philosophy.
- Governance confidence preservation framework.
- Institutional accountability continuity standards.
- Legitimacy erosion prevention doctrine.
- Civilization trust survivability architecture.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No legitimacy or trust layer creates runtime authority.
- No legitimacy verification bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No confidence layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No trust doctrine weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows meta-governance containment and coherence preservation.
- Safe to include now: legitimacy preservation, trust continuity, transparency standards, credibility architecture, accountability standards, and erosion prevention.
- Future upgrade: public accountability packet and transparency review template doctrine.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated legitimacy scoring systems, trust-manipulation systems, or execution systems.
